focused_output: d1 — Let n be collection size and m be lookup count. Building either representation costs Θ(n) under the supplied expected-hashing model. Linear-list membership costs Θ(n) per miss or worst-case lookup, giving Θ(n+mn); one-time set construction plus expected Θ(1) membership gives expected Θ(n+m). For m=100, these totals are Θ(101n) and expected Θ(n+100), respectively.

d2 — With m fixed at 100, both totals are Θ(n) as functions of n alone; the set offers an expected operation-count/constant-factor advantage, not a different one-parameter asymptotic class. With both n and m variable, Θ(n+m) reaches the Ω(n+m) lower bound for consuming n elements and answering m queries, so the expected hash-table solution is converged under this model.

d3 — The advantage requires constructing the set once, hashable keys with equality-consistent and stable hashes, and a bounded-load approximately uniform hashing model. Adversarial collisions make each set lookup Θ(n), restoring Θ(n+mn); rebuilding the set for every lookup likewise costs expected Θ(mn). The set also concedes Θ(n) additional transient space when converted from an existing list.

Lens sweep — 1 relational: not applicable, no relation or dependency design; 2 concurrency: not applicable, no shared schedule; 3 distributed: not applicable, no replication; 4 complexity: fired for construction, lookup, lower bound, and convergence; 5 type/formal methods: fired for the hash/equality and key-stability representation invariants; 6 information theory: fired because hash distribution and collisions determine expected versus worst-case cost; 7 architecture: fired only for reversibility—the transient substitution has local blast radius and no persistent commitment.

Empirical closure — pending for any wall-clock claim: belief=one-time set construction plus 100 lookups beats list construction plus 100 scans at representative n; prediction=lower median end-to-end runtime for the set path; disconfirming_observation=list time is equal or lower; test=bounded benchmark including construction with representative hit/miss positions and key types; prediction_recorded_before_result=true; result and update=pending.

Verdict — c1 is established. Prefer the transient set for this 100-lookup script only under the stated one-build operation mix and expected-hashing invariants; retain the list when n is small, lookups are early hits, hashing is expensive or unsuitable, memory is constrained, or measurement disconfirms the expected constant-factor gain.
